_note: Override DDB-synced language data with campaign-specific assignments. Keys are player IDs (kim, jerome, ed, jen, nick) — these match character-assignments.json.

languages.note: Unknown to all Europeans. Her mother's language.

kim.languageNote: Zarina's Elvish is from the Americas — completely unknown to any European NPC. To them it sounds like no language they have encountered. Her Draconic will be useful at all three castles — old records kept in Draconic at Orava, binding-script elements at Houska.

jerome.languageNote: Barry speaks Common only. As a Van Helsing bloodline descendant he may have encountered other languages in his family records — this can surface as an arc beat. Complete barrier with all local languages.

ed.playerNote: Spurt is currently played by AI but has the same character data slot.

languages.note: Universal dragon tongue. Spurt thinks in Draconic.

ed.languageNote: Spurt is from the Americas but speaks Common and Draconic. Draconic being universal means he can speak it anywhere — it predates human civilization on every continent. Vladislav recognizes it immediately when Spurt speaks it.

spurt._alias: Same as ed — Spurt AI player slot.

languages.note: Universal dragon tongue. Spurt thinks in Draconic.

spurt.languageNote: Spurt is from the Americas. Speaks Common and Draconic. Draconic is universal.

languages.note: 40 years in Central Europe. First European language he learned.
Picked up on the road through Poland.
Enough to understand Marta when she switches to Slovak if frightened. Enough to catch Gregor's warnings without Katya.

Chazz can read and understand Infernal without having formally learned it. He does not know he can do this. First revealed at Houska when he reads binding-script before realizing he is reading it. Max whispers DM when this triggers: 'Chazz can read this. Watch.'

nick.languageNote: Chazz has been in Europe for 40 years — he is the party's primary translator for Central European languages. He gets information first when translating. His Infernal literacy is instinctive and unknown to him — a tiefling bloodline gift he has never had reason to discover.

languages.note: Shared with Zarina — their private channel. Unknown to Europeans.
Firbolg heritage language. No Giant speakers in Central Europe but old Giant inscriptions exist in remote locations.

Communicates with beasts and plants regardless of language. Animals understand her intent. She understands their emotion and intent not words. Complete barrier with all European languages except Common.

jen.languageNote: Marfire arrived 3 months ago and has not acquired European languages. Complete barrier with Slovak, Czech, German, Hungarian. Her shared Elvish with Zarina is their private channel — Vladislav understands Elvish and will not reveal this. Her Speech of Beast and Leaf delivers animal intelligence no one else can access.
